rationale: Title Y is more precise, discloses both key findings (discrimination and PVI confirmation), follows Heart Rhythm's title-case-with-subtitle convention, and explicitly states the prospective study design, making it substantially more informative and appealing.
loser_weakness: Title X omits the second finding (PVI confirmation), uses an informal lowercase sentence style atypical of HR, and lacks any study-design descriptor, leaving readers with an incomplete picture of the study's scope.
